title: ISigmaSeparation
lean_code_with_comments:
set_option linter.unusedVariables false in
-- claim_3_25 -- start statement
theorem iSigmaSeparation_marginalize_iff
    (G : CDMG Node) (A B C : Set Node) (D : Finset Node) (hD_sub : D ⊆ G.V)
    (hA : A ⊆ (↑G.J : Set Node) ∪ ↑G.V)
    (hB : B ⊆ (↑G.J : Set Node) ∪ ↑G.V)
    (hC : C ⊆ (↑G.J : Set Node) ∪ ↑G.V)
    (hD_disj : Disjoint (↑D : Set Node) (A ∪ B ∪ C)) :
    G.IsISigmaSeparated A B C hA hB hC ↔
      (G.marginalize D hD_sub).IsISigmaSeparated A B C
        (G.subset_marginalize_carrier_of_disjoint A D hD_sub hA
          (hD_disj.mono_right
            (Set.subset_union_left.trans Set.subset_union_left)))
        (G.subset_marginalize_carrier_of_disjoint B D hD_sub hB
          (hD_disj.mono_right
            (Set.subset_union_right.trans Set.subset_union_left)))
        (G.subset_marginalize_carrier_of_disjoint C D hD_sub hC
          (hD_disj.mono_right Set.subset_union_right))
-- claim_3_25 -- end statement
:= by
  -- ## Step 1 (LN tex) — reduction to one-node marginalization.
  --
  -- Outer induction on `D.card`.  The IH needs to apply at a
  -- different CDMG (`G.marginalize {u} hu` for some `u ∈ D`) with a
  -- different argument set (`D.erase u`), so we promote the iff to a
  -- form universally quantified over the CDMG `G'` and the Finset
  -- `D'` before inducting on `n = D'.card`.
  suffices h : ∀ (n : ℕ) (G' : CDMG Node)
      (hA' : A ⊆ (↑G'.J : Set Node) ∪ ↑G'.V)
      (hB' : B ⊆ (↑G'.J : Set Node) ∪ ↑G'.V)
      (hC' : C ⊆ (↑G'.J : Set Node) ∪ ↑G'.V)
      (D' : Finset Node) (hD'_sub : D' ⊆ G'.V)
      (hD'_disj : Disjoint (↑D' : Set Node) (A ∪ B ∪ C)),
      D'.card = n →
      (G'.IsISigmaSeparated A B C hA' hB' hC' ↔
        (G'.marginalize D' hD'_sub).IsISigmaSeparated A B C
          (G'.subset_marginalize_carrier_of_disjoint A D' hD'_sub hA'
            (hD'_disj.mono_right
              (Set.subset_union_left.trans Set.subset_union_left)))
          (G'.subset_marginalize_carrier_of_disjoint B D' hD'_sub hB'
            (hD'_disj.mono_right
              (Set.subset_union_right.trans Set.subset_union_left)))
          (G'.subset_marginalize_carrier_of_disjoint C D' hD'_sub hC'
            (hD'_disj.mono_right Set.subset_union_right))) by
    exact h D.card G hA hB hC D hD_sub hD_disj rfl
  intro n
  induction n with
  | zero =>
      -- Base case: D'.card = 0 ⇒ D' = ∅; reduce to
      -- `iSigmaSeparation_marginalize_empty_iff`.
      intro G' _ _ _ D' hD'_sub hD'_disj h_card
      have hD'_empty : D' = ∅ := Finset.card_eq_zero.mp h_card
      subst hD'_empty
      exact iSigmaSeparation_marginalize_empty_iff G' A B C hD'_sub _ _ _ _ _ _
  | succ n ih =>
      -- Inductive step: D'.card = n + 1 ⇒ ∃ u ∈ D'.  Let E := D'.erase u
      -- (E.card = n).  Chain:
      --   G'.IsISigmaSep A B C
      --     ↔[one_iff at u]
      --   (G'.marginalize {u} hu_sub).IsISigmaSep A B C
      --     ↔[ih at G'.marginalize {u} hu_sub, E]
      --   ((G'.marginalize {u} hu_sub).marginalize E hE_sub_marg).IsISigmaSep A B C
      --     ↔[iSigmaSeparated_iff_of_eq via marginalize_comm]
      --   (G'.marginalize D' hD'_sub).IsISigmaSep A B C
      intro G' hA' hB' hC' D' hD'_sub hD'_disj h_card
      have hD'_ne : D'.Nonempty := Finset.card_pos.mp (by omega)
      obtain ⟨u, hu_in_D'⟩ := hD'_ne
      let E : Finset Node := D'.erase u
      have hE_card : E.card = n := by
        change (D'.erase u).card = n
        have h := Finset.card_erase_add_one hu_in_D'
        omega
      -- {u} ⊆ G'.V from u ∈ D' ⊆ G'.V.
      have hu_sub : ({u} : Finset Node) ⊆ G'.V := by
        intro x hx
        rw [Finset.mem_singleton] at hx
        subst hx
        exact hD'_sub hu_in_D'
      have hu_notin_E : u ∉ E := Finset.notMem_erase u D'
      -- E ⊆ (G'.marginalize {u} hu_sub).V (= G'.V \ {u}) via u ∉ E.
      have hE_sub_marg : E ⊆ (G'.marginalize {u} hu_sub).V := by
        change E ⊆ G'.V \ {u}
        intro x hx
        rw [Finset.mem_sdiff, Finset.mem_singleton]
        refine ⟨hD'_sub (Finset.mem_of_mem_erase hx), ?_⟩
        intro h_eq
        subst h_eq
        exact hu_notin_E hx
      have hE_sub_G' : E ⊆ G'.V :=
        fun x hx => hD'_sub (Finset.mem_of_mem_erase hx)
      -- {u} ∩ (A ∪ B ∪ C) = ∅, derived from D' ∩ (A ∪ B ∪ C) = ∅ and
      -- {u} ⊆ D' (Set-coercion side).
      have hu_in_D'_coe : (↑({u} : Finset Node) : Set Node) ⊆ ↑D' := by
        rw [Finset.coe_subset, Finset.singleton_subset_iff]
        exact hu_in_D'
      have hu_disj_ABC : Disjoint (↑({u} : Finset Node) : Set Node)
                                  (A ∪ B ∪ C) :=
        hD'_disj.mono_left hu_in_D'_coe
      -- E ∩ (A ∪ B ∪ C) = ∅ from E ⊆ D'.
      have hE_sub_D'_coe : (↑E : Set Node) ⊆ ↑D' :=
        Finset.coe_subset.mpr (Finset.erase_subset u D')
      have hE_disj_ABC : Disjoint (↑E : Set Node) (A ∪ B ∪ C) :=
        hD'_disj.mono_left hE_sub_D'_coe
      -- Carrier transports on G'.marginalize {u} hu_sub for A, B, C.
      have hA_marg : A ⊆ (↑(G'.marginalize {u} hu_sub).J : Set Node) ∪
                         ↑(G'.marginalize {u} hu_sub).V :=
        G'.subset_marginalize_carrier_of_disjoint A {u} hu_sub hA'
          (hu_disj_ABC.mono_right
            (Set.subset_union_left.trans Set.subset_union_left))
      have hB_marg : B ⊆ (↑(G'.marginalize {u} hu_sub).J : Set Node) ∪
                         ↑(G'.marginalize {u} hu_sub).V :=
        G'.subset_marginalize_carrier_of_disjoint B {u} hu_sub hB'
          (hu_disj_ABC.mono_right
            (Set.subset_union_right.trans Set.subset_union_left))
      have hC_marg : C ⊆ (↑(G'.marginalize {u} hu_sub).J : Set Node) ∪
                         ↑(G'.marginalize {u} hu_sub).V :=
        G'.subset_marginalize_carrier_of_disjoint C {u} hu_sub hC'
          (hu_disj_ABC.mono_right Set.subset_union_right)
      -- IH at (G'.marginalize {u} hu_sub, E).
      have step_ih := ih (G'.marginalize {u} hu_sub) hA_marg hB_marg hC_marg
                          E hE_sub_marg hE_disj_ABC hE_card
      -- One-node iff at (G', u).
      have step_one := iSigmaSeparation_marginalize_one_iff G' u hu_sub A B C
                          hA' hB' hC' hu_disj_ABC
      -- The CDMG equality:
      --   (G'.marginalize {u} hu_sub).marginalize E hE_sub_marg
      --     = G'.marginalize D' hD'_sub.
      -- Via marginalize_comm + the union identity {u} ∪ E = D'.
      have h_uE_disj : Disjoint ({u} : Finset Node) E := by
        rw [Finset.disjoint_singleton_left]
        exact hu_notin_E
      have h_union_eq : ({u} : Finset Node) ∪ E = D' := by
        ext x
        simp only [Finset.mem_union, Finset.mem_singleton, Finset.mem_erase, E]
        constructor
        · rintro (rfl | ⟨_, hx⟩)
          · exact hu_in_D'
          · exact hx
        · intro hx
          by_cases h : x = u
          · exact Or.inl h
          · exact Or.inr ⟨h, hx⟩
      have h_marg_chain :
          (G'.marginalize {u} hu_sub).marginalize E hE_sub_marg
            = G'.marginalize D' hD'_sub := by
        -- Bridge marginalize_comm's `G'.marginalize ({u} ∪ E) _` to
        -- `G'.marginalize D' hD'_sub` via the union identity
        -- `{u} ∪ E = D'`.  Direct `rw` on the goal fails (the subset
        -- proof's type depends on the Finset arg, so the motive is
        -- not type-correct); instead we `clear_value` the let-binding
        -- for `E` and then `subst` to substitute D' := {u} ∪ E,
        -- after which proof irrelevance closes the residual.
        have h_comm := (marginalize_comm G' {u} E hu_sub hE_sub_G' h_uE_disj).1
        clear_value E
        subst h_union_eq
        exact h_comm
      -- Transport the iff endpoint via h_marg_chain.
      have step_eq := iSigmaSeparated_iff_of_eq (A := A) (B := B) (C := C)
        h_marg_chain
        ((G'.marginalize {u} hu_sub).subset_marginalize_carrier_of_disjoint A E
          hE_sub_marg hA_marg
          (hE_disj_ABC.mono_right
            (Set.subset_union_left.trans Set.subset_union_left)))
        ((G'.marginalize {u} hu_sub).subset_marginalize_carrier_of_disjoint B E
          hE_sub_marg hB_marg
          (hE_disj_ABC.mono_right
            (Set.subset_union_right.trans Set.subset_union_left)))
        ((G'.marginalize {u} hu_sub).subset_marginalize_carrier_of_disjoint C E
          hE_sub_marg hC_marg
          (hE_disj_ABC.mono_right Set.subset_union_right))
        (G'.subset_marginalize_carrier_of_disjoint A D' hD'_sub hA'
          (hD'_disj.mono_right
            (Set.subset_union_left.trans Set.subset_union_left)))
        (G'.subset_marginalize_carrier_of_disjoint B D' hD'_sub hB'
          (hD'_disj.mono_right
            (Set.subset_union_right.trans Set.subset_union_left)))
        (G'.subset_marginalize_carrier_of_disjoint C D' hD'_sub hC'
          (hD'_disj.mono_right Set.subset_union_right))
      exact step_one.trans (step_ih.trans step_eq)
lean_code_without_comments:
set_option linter.unusedVariables false in

theorem iSigmaSeparation_marginalize_iff
    (G : CDMG Node) (A B C : Set Node) (D : Finset Node) (hD_sub : D ⊆ G.V)
    (hA : A ⊆ (↑G.J : Set Node) ∪ ↑G.V)
    (hB : B ⊆ (↑G.J : Set Node) ∪ ↑G.V)
    (hC : C ⊆ (↑G.J : Set Node) ∪ ↑G.V)
    (hD_disj : Disjoint (↑D : Set Node) (A ∪ B ∪ C)) :
    G.IsISigmaSeparated A B C hA hB hC ↔
      (G.marginalize D hD_sub).IsISigmaSeparated A B C
        (G.subset_marginalize_carrier_of_disjoint A D hD_sub hA
          (hD_disj.mono_right
            (Set.subset_union_left.trans Set.subset_union_left)))
        (G.subset_marginalize_carrier_of_disjoint B D hD_sub hB
          (hD_disj.mono_right
            (Set.subset_union_right.trans Set.subset_union_left)))
        (G.subset_marginalize_carrier_of_disjoint C D hD_sub hC
          (hD_disj.mono_right Set.subset_union_right))

:= by

  suffices h : ∀ (n : ℕ) (G' : CDMG Node)
      (hA' : A ⊆ (↑G'.J : Set Node) ∪ ↑G'.V)
      (hB' : B ⊆ (↑G'.J : Set Node) ∪ ↑G'.V)
      (hC' : C ⊆ (↑G'.J : Set Node) ∪ ↑G'.V)
      (D' : Finset Node) (hD'_sub : D' ⊆ G'.V)
      (hD'_disj : Disjoint (↑D' : Set Node) (A ∪ B ∪ C)),
      D'.card = n →
      (G'.IsISigmaSeparated A B C hA' hB' hC' ↔
        (G'.marginalize D' hD'_sub).IsISigmaSeparated A B C
          (G'.subset_marginalize_carrier_of_disjoint A D' hD'_sub hA'
            (hD'_disj.mono_right
              (Set.subset_union_left.trans Set.subset_union_left)))
          (G'.subset_marginalize_carrier_of_disjoint B D' hD'_sub hB'
            (hD'_disj.mono_right
              (Set.subset_union_right.trans Set.subset_union_left)))
          (G'.subset_marginalize_carrier_of_disjoint C D' hD'_sub hC'
            (hD'_disj.mono_right Set.subset_union_right))) by
    exact h D.card G hA hB hC D hD_sub hD_disj rfl
  intro n
  induction n with
  | zero =>

      intro G' _ _ _ D' hD'_sub hD'_disj h_card
      have hD'_empty : D' = ∅ := Finset.card_eq_zero.mp h_card
      subst hD'_empty
      exact iSigmaSeparation_marginalize_empty_iff G' A B C hD'_sub _ _ _ _ _ _
  | succ n ih =>

      intro G' hA' hB' hC' D' hD'_sub hD'_disj h_card
      have hD'_ne : D'.Nonempty := Finset.card_pos.mp (by omega)
      obtain ⟨u, hu_in_D'⟩ := hD'_ne
      let E : Finset Node := D'.erase u
      have hE_card : E.card = n := by
        change (D'.erase u).card = n
        have h := Finset.card_erase_add_one hu_in_D'
        omega

      have hu_sub : ({u} : Finset Node) ⊆ G'.V := by
        intro x hx
        rw [Finset.mem_singleton] at hx
        subst hx
        exact hD'_sub hu_in_D'
      have hu_notin_E : u ∉ E := Finset.notMem_erase u D'

      have hE_sub_marg : E ⊆ (G'.marginalize {u} hu_sub).V := by
        change E ⊆ G'.V \ {u}
        intro x hx
        rw [Finset.mem_sdiff, Finset.mem_singleton]
        refine ⟨hD'_sub (Finset.mem_of_mem_erase hx), ?_⟩
        intro h_eq
        subst h_eq
        exact hu_notin_E hx
      have hE_sub_G' : E ⊆ G'.V :=
        fun x hx => hD'_sub (Finset.mem_of_mem_erase hx)

      have hu_in_D'_coe : (↑({u} : Finset Node) : Set Node) ⊆ ↑D' := by
        rw [Finset.coe_subset, Finset.singleton_subset_iff]
        exact hu_in_D'
      have hu_disj_ABC : Disjoint (↑({u} : Finset Node) : Set Node)
                                  (A ∪ B ∪ C) :=
        hD'_disj.mono_left hu_in_D'_coe

      have hE_sub_D'_coe : (↑E : Set Node) ⊆ ↑D' :=
        Finset.coe_subset.mpr (Finset.erase_subset u D')
      have hE_disj_ABC : Disjoint (↑E : Set Node) (A ∪ B ∪ C) :=
        hD'_disj.mono_left hE_sub_D'_coe

      have hA_marg : A ⊆ (↑(G'.marginalize {u} hu_sub).J : Set Node) ∪
                         ↑(G'.marginalize {u} hu_sub).V :=
        G'.subset_marginalize_carrier_of_disjoint A {u} hu_sub hA'
          (hu_disj_ABC.mono_right
            (Set.subset_union_left.trans Set.subset_union_left))
      have hB_marg : B ⊆ (↑(G'.marginalize {u} hu_sub).J : Set Node) ∪
                         ↑(G'.marginalize {u} hu_sub).V :=
        G'.subset_marginalize_carrier_of_disjoint B {u} hu_sub hB'
          (hu_disj_ABC.mono_right
            (Set.subset_union_right.trans Set.subset_union_left))
      have hC_marg : C ⊆ (↑(G'.marginalize {u} hu_sub).J : Set Node) ∪
                         ↑(G'.marginalize {u} hu_sub).V :=
        G'.subset_marginalize_carrier_of_disjoint C {u} hu_sub hC'
          (hu_disj_ABC.mono_right Set.subset_union_right)

      have step_ih := ih (G'.marginalize {u} hu_sub) hA_marg hB_marg hC_marg
                          E hE_sub_marg hE_disj_ABC hE_card

      have step_one := iSigmaSeparation_marginalize_one_iff G' u hu_sub A B C
                          hA' hB' hC' hu_disj_ABC

      have h_uE_disj : Disjoint ({u} : Finset Node) E := by
        rw [Finset.disjoint_singleton_left]
        exact hu_notin_E
      have h_union_eq : ({u} : Finset Node) ∪ E = D' := by
        ext x
        simp only [Finset.mem_union, Finset.mem_singleton, Finset.mem_erase, E]
        constructor
        · rintro (rfl | ⟨_, hx⟩)
          · exact hu_in_D'
          · exact hx
        · intro hx
          by_cases h : x = u
          · exact Or.inl h
          · exact Or.inr ⟨h, hx⟩
      have h_marg_chain :
          (G'.marginalize {u} hu_sub).marginalize E hE_sub_marg
            = G'.marginalize D' hD'_sub := by

        have h_comm := (marginalize_comm G' {u} E hu_sub hE_sub_G' h_uE_disj).1
        clear_value E
        subst h_union_eq
        exact h_comm

      have step_eq := iSigmaSeparated_iff_of_eq (A := A) (B := B) (C := C)
        h_marg_chain
        ((G'.marginalize {u} hu_sub).subset_marginalize_carrier_of_disjoint A E
          hE_sub_marg hA_marg
          (hE_disj_ABC.mono_right
            (Set.subset_union_left.trans Set.subset_union_left)))
        ((G'.marginalize {u} hu_sub).subset_marginalize_carrier_of_disjoint B E
          hE_sub_marg hB_marg
          (hE_disj_ABC.mono_right
            (Set.subset_union_right.trans Set.subset_union_left)))
        ((G'.marginalize {u} hu_sub).subset_marginalize_carrier_of_disjoint C E
          hE_sub_marg hC_marg
          (hE_disj_ABC.mono_right Set.subset_union_right))
        (G'.subset_marginalize_carrier_of_disjoint A D' hD'_sub hA'
          (hD'_disj.mono_right
            (Set.subset_union_left.trans Set.subset_union_left)))
        (G'.subset_marginalize_carrier_of_disjoint B D' hD'_sub hB'
          (hD'_disj.mono_right
            (Set.subset_union_right.trans Set.subset_union_left)))
        (G'.subset_marginalize_carrier_of_disjoint C D' hD'_sub hC'
          (hD'_disj.mono_right Set.subset_union_right))
      exact step_one.trans (step_ih.trans step_eq)
lean_explanation:
The theorem `iSigmaSeparation_marginalize_iff` formalises the LN's $i\sigma$-separation-under-marginalization biconditional as a single `Iff` between two evaluations of the same predicate on two different CDMGs.

The ambient binder block `{Node : Type*} [DecidableEq Node]` is the chapter-wide convention: `Node` is the polymorphic vertex type, and `DecidableEq` is needed for the `Finset Node`-valued fields of `CDMG`. `G : CDMG Node` is the LN's $G = (J, V, E, L)$ — a conditional directed mixed graph (`def_3_15`). The three argument sets `A B C : Set Node` carry subset hypotheses `hA, hB, hC : · ⊆ ↑G.J ∪ ↑G.V` matching the LN's $A, B, C \subseteq J \cup V$. The marginalization target `D : Finset Node` is typed as a `Finset` (not a `Set`) because `def_3_14`'s `marginalize : (G : CDMG Node) (W : Finset Node) (hW : W ⊆ G.V) → CDMG Node` takes a `Finset`; `hD_sub : D ⊆ G.V` is its precondition (the LN's $D \subseteq V$).

A few Mathlib idioms are worth flagging. `↑D : Set Node` is the `Finset → Set` coercion, used only in the disjointness premise where the two carriers must meet. `Disjoint (↑D : Set Node) (A ∪ B ∪ C)` is Mathlib's `Disjoint` predicate — propositionally equivalent to the LN's $D \cap (A \cup B \cup C) = \emptyset$ via `Set.disjoint_iff_inter_eq_empty`, but pairs more cleanly with the `Disjoint.mono_right` slicing API. `G.IsISigmaSeparated A B C hA hB hC` is the $i\sigma$-separation predicate of `def_3_18` — the same predicate appears on both sides of the `Iff`, just evaluated on different CDMGs. `G.marginalize D hD_sub` is the marginalized graph $G^{\smallsetminus D}$.

The shape of the encoding is: hypotheses (the CDMG, the three node sets, the marginalization target, the four subset / disjointness conditions); conclusion is a single `Iff` whose left-hand side is `G.IsISigmaSeparated A B C hA hB hC` and whose right-hand side is the same predicate on `G.marginalize D hD_sub` with the three carrier-transported subset proofs inlined as calls to the helper `subset_marginalize_carrier_of_disjoint`. The proof body inducts on `D.card`, peeling one node at a time via `iSigmaSeparation_marginalize_one_iff` and the commutativity of marginalization (`claim_3_17`).
design_choices:
**Single `theorem` mirroring the LN's single biconditional.** The LN's `\begin{Lem}` block bundles the statement as one equivalence between two $i\sigma$-separation predicates, with the same triple `(A, B, C)` reinterpreted on two CDMGs. Encoding it as one `theorem iSigmaSeparation_marginalize_iff` whose conclusion is a single `Iff` is the only LN-faithful shape; an `And`-bundled variant carrying extra side properties or a multi-item split was considered and rejected because the LN exposes no sub-bullets and no separately quotable sub-claims here.

**Same `IsISigmaSeparated` predicate on both sides of the `Iff`.** All marginalization-specific work lives in the *graph* slot (`G` vs `G.marginalize D hD_sub`); the $i\sigma$-separation predicate of `def_3_18` is reused verbatim, with no parallel "marginalized version" and no inlining of the predicate's walk-blocking content into the conclusion. A bespoke `IsISigmaSeparated_OnMarginalized` wrapping the marginalize step was considered and rejected — it would duplicate `def_3_18`, and the LN itself treats $G^{\smallsetminus D}$ as just another CDMG on which the *same* predicate is evaluated.

**Asymmetric typing: `A, B, C : Set Node` vs `D : Finset Node`.** This mirrors the LN's asymmetric typing $A, B, C \subseteq J \cup V$ vs $D \subseteq V$. The math reason is that marginalization is only defined when its target lies in the output-node set $V$ (per `def_3_14`), whereas $i\sigma$-separation admits arbitrary node-subsets of $J \cup V$ (per `def_3_18`). The Lean-mechanical consequence is dictated by the upstream APIs: `marginalize` takes a `Finset Node` (because `G.V` itself is a `Finset Node`), while `IsISigmaSeparated` takes `Set Node`. The `Finset → Set` coercion `↑D` is used only in the disjointness premise where the two carriers must meet.

**`Disjoint`-shaped disjointness premise.** Propositionally equivalent to the LN's $D \cap (A \cup B \cup C) = \emptyset$ via `Set.disjoint_iff_inter_eq_empty`, but `Disjoint` is the Mathlib-idiomatic phrasing and pairs cleanly with the `Disjoint.mono_right` slicing used to extract the three per-argument disjointness premises `Disjoint ↑D A`, `Disjoint ↑D B`, `Disjoint ↑D C` consumed by the well-typedness helper.

**Three transported well-typedness witnesses inlined into the conclusion.** The right-hand side calls `(G.marginalize D hD_sub).IsISigmaSeparated A B C _ _ _` where the three holes are transported subset hypotheses for $A, B, C$ against the $G^{\smallsetminus D}$ carrier, each produced by a call to the helper `subset_marginalize_carrier_of_disjoint`. Promoting them to named hypotheses was considered and rejected: the LN treats the well-typedness of $A, B, C$ against the marginalized carrier as a derivable consequence of the disjointness premise (cf.\ the LN's "Well-typedness of $A, B, C$" paragraph), so inlining preserves the LN's reading and keeps the well-typedness inside the statement.
